text:
## AWS Audit Report — ap-southeast-1 (Account 442740305558)

### 1. Identity
- **User**: `bot-admin-Global-management`
- **Account**: `442740305558`

### 2. EC2 Instances
- **None running.** Zero instances in ap-southeast-1.
- **Instance i-037b78bd5232103a9 (vault-al2023-arm) does NOT exist** — fully terminated/deleted.

### 3. VPCs
| VPC ID | CIDR | Default | Name |
|--------|------|---------|------|
| vpc-0b4c0e08c64f257a2 | 172.31.0.0/16 | Yes | (none) |

Only the default VPC exists.

### 4. Subnets
| Subnet ID | AZ | CIDR |
|-----------|-----|------|
| subnet-021cb79acb7496858 | ap-southeast-1b | 172.31.16.0/20 |
| subnet-092699323945160c8 | ap-southeast-1a | 172.31.32.0/20 |
| subnet-050ceae6651a7b5c5 | ap-southeast-1c | 172.31.0.0/20 |

All default subnets (3 AZs).

### 5. Security Groups
| SG ID | Name | VPC |
|-------|------|-----|
| sg-0878a0b6f510523e5 | default | vpc-0b4c0e08c64f257a2 |

Only the default SG exists.

### 6. S3 Buckets
**None.** No S3 buckets in this account.

### 7. RDS
**None.** No RDS instances.

### 8. EKS
**None.** No EKS clusters.

### 9. Route53 — Hosted Zones
| Zone | ID | Records |
|------|----|---------|
| <your-domain>. | Z049947528RQWVBXKDTLX | 48 |

**No acaps.net zone found** in this account.

### 10. Route53 — Key DNS Records for sg-prod-1

| Record | Type | Value |
|--------|------|-------|
| **<server>.<your-domain>** | **A** | **115.66.61.34** |
| **<short-name>.<your-domain>** | **CNAME** | **<server>.<your-domain>** |
| <traefik>.<your-domain> | A | 115.66.61.34 |
| <registry>.<your-domain> | A | 115.66.61.34 |
| <n8n>.<your-domain> | A | 115.66.61.34 |
| <whoami>.<your-domain> | A | 115.66.61.34 |
| <your-domain> (root) | A | 18.142.227.94 |

**sg-prod-1 IP is 115.66.61.34** — 5 DNS records point to it (sg-prod-1, sg, traefik, docker, n8n, whoami).

### Other notable DNS records:
| Record | Type | Value | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|-------|
| <ai>.<your-domain> | A | 54.255.36.25 | |
| <ca>.<your-domain> | A | 13.229.209.166 | |
| <consul>.<your-domain> | A | 139.224.233.161 | Alibaba Cloud IP |
| <elastic>.<your-domain> | A | 18.141.179.152 | Old services? |
| <v>.<your-domain> | A | 13.214.115.188 | |
| <vault>.<your-domain> | A | 54.169.236.92 | Old vault? |
| <ts>.<your-domain> | A | 139.224.233.161 | Alibaba Cloud IP |
| Multiple registry mirrors (gcr, ghcr, mcr, etc.) | A | 18.141.179.152 | All same IP |

### 11. IAM Users
| User | Created |
|------|---------|
| bot-admin-Global-management | 2025-07-29 |
| bot-ddns-route53 | 2026-02-10 |

### 12. IAM Roles
- AWSReservedSSO_AdministratorAccess_bbfcc86e1bc757dd
- AWSReservedSSO_PowerUserAccess_a6ae9d3f9708d6a5
- OrganizationAccountAccessRole
- Share-peter-<your-domain>
- Share_DNS_AWS-CN-Production

### 13. KMS Keys
5 keys exist (IDs: 520c12dc, b8ef9b51, c466bef8, dca65161, f0ba332a)

### 14. DynamoDB
**None.**

### 15. Elastic IPs / EBS Volumes / Load Balancers
**All empty.** No EIPs, EBS volumes, or ALBs.

---

## Summary

The AWS account is **very clean** — essentially just:
- Default VPC with default subnets/SG
- Route53 zone for <your-domain> (48 records)
- 2 IAM users (bot-admin, bot-ddns-route53)
- 5 IAM roles (SSO + org + sharing)
- 5 KMS keys
- **No compute resources** (no EC2, EKS, RDS, ALBs)

**Key finding: sg-prod-1 (115.66.61.34) is NOT an AWS resource** — it's an external server. The vault instance from aws-cleanup.md is fully gone.

**Stale DNS concern**: Many A records (18.141.179.152 for registry mirrors, 139.224.233.161 for consul/ts, etc.) may point to defunct infrastructure that should be cleaned up.
summary: Complete AWS audit - sg-prod-1 IP is 115.66.61.34, account is clean

text:
## sg-prod-1 Server Audit - Complete Findings

### CRITICAL: Server is NOT reachable from the internet

SSH to 115.66.61.34 fails on ALL ports tested (22, 80, 443, 2222, 6443, 8443, 51820). Ping also fails (100% packet loss). The server has a **private IP (<lan-ip>)** on its NIC and the public IP (115.66.61.34) has **no port forwarding configured** at the router/firewall level.

The only access is via **iKVM/BMC console at 10.1.1.241** (noVNC web interface on the local network).

---

### 1. OS & System (from iKVM screenshots dated Feb 6, 2026)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **OS** | Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS |
| **Kernel** | 6.8.0-94-generic x86_64 |
| **Hostname** | `finclab-us1` (NOT sg-prod-1 - needs renaming) |
| **Install date** | Feb 6, 2026 |
| **Status** | Fresh install, minimal configuration |
| **System load** | 0.12 |
| **Temperature** | 77.8°C |
| **Processes** | 2486 |

### 2. Hardware / Storage

**Boot/OS Drive:**
- 1x Samsung SSD 970 EVO Plus 2TB NVMe — 1.819T
  - Partition 1: ESP (vfat) 512MB
  - LVM VG `vgubuntu` (1.818T):
    - `root` LV: 1.81T ext4 (0.6% used)
    - `swap_1` LV: 976MB

**Data Array (RAID 6):**
- 6x Samsung SSD 860 EVO 4TB SATA — 3.638T each
- Software RAID 6 as `md127` — 21.83T raw (~14.5T usable)
- **NOT MOUNTED** after fresh install (needs manual mount/LVM setup)

### 3. Network

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **NIC** | enp68s0 |
| **Private IPv4** | <lan-ip> |
| **Public IP (DNS)** | 115.66.61.34 |
| **BMC/IPMI** | 10.1.1.241 |
| **Proxy** | None configured |
| **All public ports** | CLOSED/FILTERED |

**DNS records pointing to 115.66.61.34:**
- `<server>.<your-domain>` (A record)
- `<short-name>.<your-domain>` (CNAME → <server>.<your-domain>)
- `<registry>.<your-domain>`, `<n8n>.<your-domain>`, `<traefik>.<your-domain>`, `<whoami>.<your-domain>`

### 4. Users & SSH

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **User** | `peter` (created during install, has sudo) |
| **Password** | Set during install |
| **SSH** | OpenSSH installed, password auth enabled |
| **SSH Keys** | NO authorized keys configured |

### 5. Software Installed

**NOTHING beyond base Ubuntu 24.04.** This is a completely fresh install.

**NOT installed (all needed):**
- k3s / Kubernetes
- Docker / containerd
- WireGuard
- Redis
- PostgreSQL / ClickHouse
- MinIO
- Traefik / Nginx / HAProxy
- No GPU drivers (unclear if GPU hardware present)

### 6. Critical Issues / Action Items

1. **Server unreachable from internet** — Router/firewall port forwarding not configured. Need to configure at the colo's network level to forward at minimum SSH (22) and WireGuard (51820/UDP) to <lan-ip>
2. **Hostname wrong** — Currently `finclab-us1`, needs to be `sg-prod-1`
3. **RAID 6 array not mounted** — md127 (6x4TB SSDs) exists but unmounted; needs LVM setup and mount
4. **No SSH keys** — Only password auth; need to deploy keys
5. **Everything needs installing** — k3s, Docker, WireGuard, all platform services
6. **25 pending security updates** — Need apt upgrade
7. **No firewall configured** — ufw/iptables not set up

### Route53 Hosted Zone Summary

Found zone `<your-domain>` (Z049947528RQWVBXKDTLX). Key records:
- `<your-domain>` → 18.142.227.94 (AWS)
- `<ai>.<your-domain>` → 54.255.36.25
- `<k8s>.<your-domain>` → 18.141.179.152
- `<vault>.<your-domain>` → 54.169.236.92
- Multiple records point to 18.141.179.152 (likely old k8s cluster)
- acaps.net zone not in this AWS account

### Bottom Line

The server is a **freshly reinstalled bare metal box** with substantial hardware (2TB NVMe boot + 6x4TB SSD RAID 6 data array) but is currently **a blank slate**. The #1 blocker is **network accessibility** — port forwarding must be configured at the router/firewall before any remote provisioning can begin. Until then, all configuration must happen via the iKVM console at 10.1.1.241.

The server audit is already complete - I sent you the full report a moment ago. Task #1 is marked completed.

Quick summary: The server at 115.66.61.34 (<server>.<your-domain>) is **completely unreachable from the internet** - all ports closed, ping fails. From iKVM screenshots (reinstall on Feb 6), I confirmed it's a freshly installed Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS box with hostname `finclab-us1`, private IP <lan-ip> on enp68s0, 2TB NVMe boot + 6x4TB SSD RAID 6 array, and nothing installed beyond base Ubuntu. The #1 blocker is port forwarding at the router/firewall level. Full details are in my earlier message.
summary: Audit already completed, results sent earlier
